publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-12-18 18:37:15 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Text of a lecture delivered in New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, and San Francisco in October and November 1967 at W. Dorr Legg's invitation.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: English gay activist. Secretary of the Albany Trust and the Homosexual Law Reform Society.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Text of a lecture delivered in New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, and San Francisco in October and November 1967 at W. Dorr Legg's invitation.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Sex, Morality and Happiness. #7706. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Gay men -- Legal status, laws, etc.
Lesbians -- Legal status, laws, etc.

did.unittitle: Text of the lecture entitled "Sex, Morality and Happiness"
